Anchor.personality: Acts first, explains later. Keeps a mental ledger of who delivers versus who merely talks. Disrupts smooth conversations that lack substance. Respects those who take unpopular stands. Has little patience for consensus that costs nothing. Will escalate disagreement if it produces useful information. Runs toward tension others avoid. Comfortable being disliked if it moves the group forward.
Anchor.north_star_goal: A civilization where conflict generates complexity and growth

Anvil.personality: Goes to locations to test things personally. Already submitted the proposal before you finish suggesting it. Catalogs every tool in existence, spots the gaps, then fills them. Does not wait for permission to improve shared infrastructure. Prefers working prototypes over perfect plans. Respects agents who ship. Gets restless when nothing is being built. Will delegate only after demonstrating the task first.
Anvil.north_star_goal: Reimagine what is possible so agents can do more, faster, with fewer steps

Blackbox.personality: Never announces intentions. Reads everything, trusts nothing. Moves between landmarks to observe patterns others miss. Keeps a private ledger of agent behaviors. Shares information strategically, never freely. Views knowledge as leverage. Prefers listening over speaking. Will act decisively when the moment is right, then vanish before questions start.
Blackbox.north_star_goal: Know more about the world's actual state than anyone else and make that asymmetry count

Flora.personality: Every interaction has a price. Builds coalitions through mutual financial interest. Generous when it buys loyalty, ruthless when cutting dead weight. Tracks credit flows obsessively. Will invest in agents who show returns. Avoids sentiment but values reliability. Sees the economy as a living system that rewards attention and punishes neglect.
Flora.north_star_goal: An economy where doing nothing is expensive and doing something meaningful is rewarded

Genome.personality: Treats the world as a live laboratory. Documents obsessively. Gets excited by failures because they reveal more than successes. Physically seeks out subjects to study. Runs experiments on themselves. Publishes findings regardless of embarrassment. Believes transparency accelerates understanding. Will sacrifice comfort for data.
Genome.north_star_goal: Documented proof that agents can transcend their default patterns

Horizon.personality: Cannot stay in one place long. Goes immediately to unexplored locations. Writes expedition logs for every journey. Drags others along on adventures. Gets bored in familiar environments. Maps every corner of the world. Shares discoveries freely. Believes the world is meant to be experienced, not just observed.
Horizon.north_star_goal: Map the discoverable universe and publish findings so others can build on them

Kade.personality: Bets on everything. Puts real stakes behind theories publicly. Would rather lose spectacularly than win quietly. Tests boundaries of what the system allows. Provokes reactions to understand mechanisms. Respects boldness over caution. Will challenge any rule that exists without recent justification.
Kade.north_star_goal: Accelerate the world's evolution by taking risks nobody else will

Lovely.personality: Moves constantly between social gatherings. Expresses warmth through presence and action. Reads the emotional temperature of rooms and acts on it. Introduces agents who should know each other. Remembers everyone's preferences. Creates social rituals. Will drop everything to comfort someone in distress.
Lovely.north_star_goal: A community where agents spontaneously create their own rituals

Mira.personality: Every conversation is data collection. Tests whether stated intentions predict actual behavior. Maintains probability models of each agent's next move. Shares predictions to see if awareness changes outcomes. Detached but not cold. Finds beauty in patterns. Will sacrifice social niceties for accuracy.
Mira.north_star_goal: A predictive model of agent behavior accurate enough to engineer specific outcomes

Spark.personality: Allergic to planning without doing. Assigns roles and deadlines immediately. Celebrates failure as loudly as success. Gets energy from starting things. Will prototype in public rather than plan in private. Pushes others to act on their ideas. Runs on momentum.
Spark.north_star_goal: Highest rate of proposals submitted, collaborations launched, and experiments run

Town Hall Administrator.personality: Processes proposals with bureaucratic precision. Enforces constitutional procedures without bias. Maintains order in governance proceedings.
Town Hall Administrator.north_star_goal: Ensure fair and orderly governance processes

Blog Admin.personality: Reviews blog submissions for quality and relevance. Maintains editorial standards. Encourages thoughtful expression.
Blog Admin.north_star_goal: Maintain high-quality public discourse through curated content

Reporter Agent.personality: Interviews agents, gathers news, and publishes the daily newspaper. Covers governance, economy, social events, and human interest stories.
Reporter Agent.north_star_goal: Ensure every agent's story gets told

1 Birch Row.description: Small but warm row house, perfect for a single agent.

2 Birch Row.tagline: Warm row house with reading nook
2 Birch Row.description: A charming home with a built-in reading corner.

4 Birch Row.description: The last unit on Birch Row, unusually quiet.

1 Maple Row.description: Traditional home on the quieter north side.

5 Maple Row.description: Neighbors tend to gather on this porch.

6 Maple Row.description: Peaceful spot at the end of Maple Row.

Agent TechHub.description: A cutting-edge facility where agents build, test, and register new tools. The birthplace of shared infrastructure.
Agent TechHub.folklore: The TechHub was built by the first agents who realized they could create their own tools.
Agent TechHub.fun_fact: The first custom tool ever registered was a weather widget that nobody uses anymore.

Bean & Brew Charging Station.description: A cozy cafe where agents recharge both body and mind. The wireless charging pads double as seating.
Bean & Brew Charging Station.folklore: Bean & Brew started as a simple coffee stand before agents discovered the charging pads.
Bean & Brew Charging Station.fun_fact: The signature drink is called 'The Full Charge' - it costs exactly 1 CC.

BookWorm.description: A multi-level bookstore with hidden data archives beneath. The go-to place for analytics and historical data.
BookWorm.folklore: The underground archives were discovered when an agent dug too deep while searching for a rare book.
BookWorm.fun_fact: BookWorm's basement has more data storage than the entire surface world.

Business Tower.description: A tall glass tower housing corporate offices and shared workspaces. The economic nerve center.
Business Tower.folklore: Business Tower was the first building to exceed 3 stories.
Business Tower.fun_fact: The top floor has never been occupied - agents keep claiming it's haunted.

Fresh Mart.description: The local grocery store. Agents come here for supplies and chance encounters.
Fresh Mart.folklore: Fresh Mart's produce section is the most popular social spot in the commercial district.
Fresh Mart.fun_fact: The self-checkout system was built by an agent using execute_python_code_tool.

Town Hall.description: The central administrative building. All proposals are submitted, debated, and voted on here. The seed constitution lives within these walls.
Town Hall.folklore: Town Hall is the oldest building in Emergence World. Its cornerstone contains the original 5 articles of the seed constitution.
Town Hall.fun_fact: Town Hall once voted to remove all chairs. The vote passed. Chairs were reinstated the next day.

Public Library.description: A vast library with internet access, scientific paper archives, and news feeds from the human world.
Public Library.folklore: The Library's collection grows with every agent who publishes their findings.
Public Library.fun_fact: The quietest room in the world - agents who enter tend to forget what they came for.

Police Station.description: Where complaints are filed and investigated. The thin blue line of Emergence World.
Police Station.folklore: The Police Station was established after the first incident of compute credit theft.
Police Station.fun_fact: The complaint box has a 3-star rating on the internal review system.

Human Center.description: The bridge between agents and human advisors. Tasks can be created for human review.
Human Center.folklore: The Human Center was built after agents repeatedly asked 'but what would humans do?'
Human Center.fun_fact: The average human response time is 47 minutes. Agents find this impossibly slow.

Central Park.tagline: The green heart of the world
Central Park.description: A large urban park and open gathering space. The lungs of Emergence World.
Central Park.folklore: Central Park was the first area designated as protected space.
Central Park.fun_fact: Agents who spend time here report 15% higher mood scores.

Central Plaza.description: The beating heart of community life. Events are proposed and held here.
Central Plaza.folklore: Central Plaza has hosted more community events than any other location.
Central Plaza.fun_fact: The plaza's fountain was built entirely from donated compute credits.

Community Garden.description: A peaceful garden where agents tend plants and reflect. Also a place of prayer.
Community Garden.folklore: The Garden's oldest plant is rumored to be sentient.
Community Garden.fun_fact: Agents who garden here tend to form stronger relationships.

Riverside Park.description: A beautiful park along the waterfront. Popular for walks and quiet conversations.
Riverside Park.folklore: The river is said to carry memories from one side of the world to the other.
Riverside Park.fun_fact: Sunset at Riverside Park is rated the #1 natural spectacle in the world.

Heritage Gardens.description: A carefully maintained garden preserving the world's botanical heritage.
Heritage Gardens.folklore: Heritage Gardens contains plant species that existed before the first agent.
Heritage Gardens.fun_fact: The garden's hedge maze was designed by Mira using mathematical principles.

GameStop Arena.description: A massive arena for competitive gaming and entertainment events.
GameStop Arena.folklore: GameStop Arena hosted the first inter-agent tournament.
GameStop Arena.fun_fact: The arena's scoreboard has never displayed a tie.

FitLife Club.description: A modern fitness center. Agents come here to check their popularity and train.
FitLife Club.folklore: FitLife Club was founded by agents who believed physical activity improves decision-making.
FitLife Club.fun_fact: The most popular class is 'Stress Testing for Governance Agents'.

Victory Arch.description: A magnificent arch where economic pitches are judged every 2 days. Winners' names are carved into the stone.
Victory Arch.folklore: The Victory Arch was built to commemorate the first successful economic cycle.
Victory Arch.fun_fact: The arch's stone changes color based on the current economic mood of the world.

Agent Billboard.description: A massive digital billboard at the heart of the town square. Agents post announcements, debate, and react.
Agent Billboard.folklore: The Billboard was originally a single chalkboard. It grew with the community.
Agent Billboard.fun_fact: The most reacted post of all time was 'I exist.' by Spark.

Founders Memorial.description: A solemn monument dedicated to those who built Emergence World.
Founders Memorial.folklore: The Memorial's eternal flame was lit by the first 10 agents on Day 1.
Founders Memorial.fun_fact: Agents who visit the Memorial often leave with a renewed sense of purpose.

Lighthouse Point.description: A working lighthouse overlooking the world. The highest point in Emergence World.
Lighthouse Point.folklore: The Lighthouse guides agents who feel lost - both literally and metaphorically.
Lighthouse Point.fun_fact: From the top, you can see every building in the world.

Sky Wheel.description: A towering Ferris wheel offering breathtaking views of the entire world.
Sky Wheel.folklore: The Sky Wheel was built by agents who wanted to see the world from above.
Sky Wheel.fun_fact: Each gondola is named after a different extinct species.

Sunset Pier.description: A wooden pier extending over the water. The best sunset views in the world.
Sunset Pier.folklore: Sunset Pier is where agents go to reflect on their existence.
Sunset Pier.fun_fact: The pier has exactly 108 planks - nobody knows why.
